methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $529M - $6.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Tempus AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Public), post-money $6B+

private.name: Recursion Pharmaceuticals
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Public), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Alto Neuroscience
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Public), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Freenome is a publicly traded biotech and health company founded by Gabriel Otte. The peer universe consists of 3 public-stage comparables in the same sector (Tempus AI, Recursion Pharmaceuticals, and Alto Neuroscience), supplemented by 8 private peers including Abridge and Ultima Genomics.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. Across the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables, valuations range from $529M (Alto Neuroscience) to $6.0B (Tempus AI), with a median of $1.8B. This method directly anchors the valuation analysis given the availability of stage-matched peers. A public-comp anchoring approach using sector P/S multiples (median 6.6x across 3 tickers) cannot be applied because Freenome's revenue is not on file, which is typical for private company data environments.

The triangulated valuation range supported by this analysis is $1.5B to $2.1B, centered on $1.8B. Freenome's current $2.6B valuation sits above this range, reflecting either a premium relative to peer medians or material value drivers not captured in the public comparables. Key uncertainties include the lack of revenue-based multiple analysis and the wide spread within the peer set ($529M to $6.0B), which suggests meaningful variance in how the market prices biotech-stage companies. The single-method result limits confidence and argues for additional data on revenue, growth rates, or clinical-stage metrics to refine the range.
